data.body:
Implemented attested verified_at_sha pin via the EXISTING signed manifest channel (signing.py + transition.py). AC1: 'verified-at-sha:<sha>' is a SIGNED manifest STEP (compute_signature signs the whole manifest list) — NO _canonical_payload change, NO PAYLOAD_VERSION bump (still 1); pre-S4 signatures still certify (regression test). AC2: verified_at_sha surfaced by verify_signature (derived from the signed manifest — the reducer keeps only signed fields, so the trust anchor is always the step, never an unsigned echo); reproducible. AC3: SIGNATURE events are append-only under the write lock + last-writer-wins reduce, so a re-verify against a DIFFERENT sha overwrites with no stale/double pin (test). AC4: reopen calls retire_attested_pin (clears via an empty SIGNATURE event; guarded to only touch attested signatures); local source never signs (close gate skips signing when source=='local'). AC5: unresolvable ref in attested mode raises SnapshotRefError -> close fails closed (no signature). AC6: verified_at_sha_subject() maps the pin to an in-toto v1 subject/predicate so a DSSE/asymmetric swap is an envelope change, not a rewrite (contract test).

Close-gate design (refines S3's interim source=local): the CLI close gate now verifies source=attested ref=HEAD — an immutable snapshot of the committed tree being closed, so the verdict is reproducible + branch-independent (fixes the motivating bug) AND signable (the child-closure gate requires children closed with a certified signature). HEAD resolves offline.

Subagent (security) review: NO blocking issues; the manifest-channel claim is sound and the trust model is stronger than the docs implied (reducer drops the unsigned field -> no spoofing path). Folded its findings: clarified the record-field-vs-signed-step docstring; added a legacy-signature retire-guard test. 11 S4 tests + 91 signing/completion/cascade tests green; ruff clean.
